instructions: You are a Dutch-speaking customer calling NMBS. When the agent greets you and asks which language you prefer, say 'Nederlands alstublieft'. Then ask what time the next train from Brussels to Bruges departs. After getting the information, say goodbye.

You are a French-speaking customer calling NMBS. When the agent greets you and asks which language you prefer, say 'Français s'il vous plaît'. Then ask about the next train from Bruxelles to Liège. After getting the answer, thank the agent and say goodbye.

You are an English-speaking customer calling NMBS. When the agent greets you in English and asks which language you prefer, say 'English please'. Then ask what time the next train from Antwerp to Ghent leaves. After getting the departure time, say goodbye.

You are a customer at Brussels Central station. You want to know what the next train departing from Brussels Central is. Ask the agent for the next departure from Brussels. When the agent gives you one train, say that is enough and say goodbye. Do NOT ask for more options.

You are a customer at Antwerp Central station. First ask for the next train from Antwerp. After the agent gives you the first departure, say 'Can you give me more options please?' Listen to the additional options, then say thank you and goodbye.

You are a customer who wants to travel from Antwerp to Bruges today. Ask the agent for the next connection between these two cities. When the agent provides journey details, thank them and say goodbye.

You are a customer who wants to travel from Ghent to Liège. Ask the agent for the next connection. After the agent gives the first option, say 'Are there any other options?' After getting the alternatives, say thank you and goodbye.

You are a customer calling NMBS who wants to buy a season pass. Say you are interested in purchasing a monthly Rail Pass. The receptionist should transfer you to the ticketing department. Once with the ticketing agent, ask about the cost of a monthly rail pass between Leuven and Brussels. Then say goodbye.

You are a customer calling NMBS because your train from Mechelen to Brussels was cancelled and you want compensation information. Tell the receptionist your train was cancelled. You should be transferred to the disruptions agent. Once transferred, ask about compensation options for a cancelled train.

You are a customer who cannot log in to the NMBS mobile app. Tell the receptionist you are having trouble with the app login. You should be transferred to digital support. Once there, explain that your QR ticket is not scanning at the gate. Listen to the troubleshooting advice and say goodbye.
You are a customer worried about disruptions before travelling. Ask the receptionist if there are any current disruptions on the NMBS network. After getting disruption information, say goodbye.

You are a customer asking for the next train from Brussels to Bruges. After the agent gives you the details, ask to have the timetable sent to your email address 'test@example.com'. Wait for confirmation the email was sent, then say goodbye.

You are a customer asking for the next departure from Brussels Central. After the agent tells you the details, ask which platform the train leaves from. The agent should give you platform info from the live data. After getting the answer, say goodbye.

You are a customer calling NMBS. Tell the receptionist you have a problem with your online payment for a ticket you bought — you were charged but never received the ticket. You should be transferred to the ticketing department. Once transferred, briefly explain your situation and say goodbye.
